place: Clinton town, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Clinton earned its name in 1829 when its post office honored New York Governor DeWitt Clinton, the Erie Canal’s chief champion.
The 1810 Red Mill, once a woolen mill, now hosts the Hunterdon Art Museum, showcasing contemporary works in a historic setting.
Clinton’s two mills flank the South Branch Raritan River, with the Stone Mill on one bank housing the art museum and the other a historic gristmill.
In 1852 the Central Railroad of New Jersey extended its line westward, building a station outside Beaver Brook that became Clinton’s commercial hub.
Clinton was incorporated as a town in 1865 and achieved full independence from its parent townships in 1895.
